context: This chapter presents the Yama Gita, the philosophical song of Yama, the God of Death. It contains Yama's teachings on dharma, the imperishable nature of the soul, the impermanence of the body and worldly attachments, and the supreme importance of righteous living as the means to transcend death and suffering.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
यमेन कथिता गीता श्रूयतां गदतो मम।
धर्मराजस्य वचनं सर्वपापप्रणाशनम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuḍa uvāca —
Yamena kathitā Gītā śrūyatāṁ gadato mama,
Dharmarājasya vacanaṁ sarvapāpapraṇāśanam.
verses.translation: Garuda said: Hear from me the Gita spoken by Yama. The words of the King of Dharma destroy all sins.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
यमो वैवस्वतो देवो यस्तवैष प्रकीर्तितः।
धर्मराजो महाबाहुः सत्यसन्धो जितेन्द्रियः॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Yamo Vaivasvato devo yas tavaiṣa prakīrtitaḥ,
Dharmarājo mahābāhuḥ satyasandho jitendriyaḥ.
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Yama, the son of Vivasvan, is the deity whom I have described to you — the King of Dharma, mighty-armed, truthful in resolve, and master of his senses.

verses.hindi:
यम उवाच —
धर्म एव परो लोके धर्मे सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितम्।
धर्मेण क्रियते सर्वं यद्यत् पश्यति चक्षुषा॥
verses.transliteration:
Yama uvāca —
Dharma eva paro loke dharme sarvaṁ pratiṣṭhitam,
dharmeṇa kriyate sarvaṁ yad yat paśyati cakṣuṣā.
verses.translation: Yama said: Dharma alone is supreme in this world; everything is established in dharma. By dharma is accomplished all that the eye beholds.

verses.hindi:
शरीरमाद्यं खलु धर्मसाधनम्
इति स्मृत्वा तत्र धर्मं समाचरेत्।
शरीरं क्षणभङ्गुरं नरस्य
धर्मस्तु नित्यं न च नश्वरः क्वचित्॥
verses.transliteration:
Śarīram ādyaṁ khalu dharmasādhanam
iti smṛtvā tatra dharmaṁ samācaret,
śarīraṁ kṣaṇabhaṅguraṁ narasya
dharmas tu nityaṁ na ca naśvaraḥ kvacit.
verses.translation: The body is indeed the primary instrument for dharma — remembering this, one should practice righteousness. The body of a man is momentary and fragile, but dharma is eternal and never perishes.

verses.hindi:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचित्
नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे॥
verses.transliteration:
Na jāyate mriyate vā kadācit
nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ,
ajo nityaḥ śāśvato'yaṁ purāṇo
na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre.
verses.translation: The soul is never born, nor does it ever die. Having come into being, it does not cease to exist. Unborn, eternal, everlasting, and ancient — it is not slain when the body is slain.

verses.hindi:
वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय
नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि।
तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णानि
अन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही॥
verses.transliteration:
Vāsāṁsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya
navāni gṛhṇāti naro'parāṇi,
tathā śarīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇāni
anyāni saṁyāti navāni dehī.
verses.translation: Just as a person discards worn-out garments and puts on new ones, so does the embodied soul cast off worn-out bodies and enter others that are new.

verses.hindi:
नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः।
न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः॥
verses.transliteration:
Nainaṁ chindanti śastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ,
na cainaṁ kledayanty āpo na śoṣayati mārutaḥ.
verses.translation: Weapons do not cleave this soul, fire does not burn it, waters do not wet it, and the wind does not dry it.

verses.hindi:
अध्रुवेण शरीरेण प्राणिनां धर्ममाचरेत्।
ध्रुवं हित्वा अध्रुवं गृह्णन् मूढो जन्ममरणैर्भ्रमेत्॥
verses.transliteration:
Adhruveṇa śarīreṇa prāṇināṁ dharmam ācaret,
dhruvaṁ hitvā adhruvaṁ gṛhṇan mūḍho janmamaraṇair bhramet.
verses.translation: With this impermanent body, living beings should practice dharma. The fool who abandons the permanent and grasps the impermanent wanders through the cycle of birth and death.

verses.hindi:
यथा ह्यग्निं प्रकाशं च समिद्भिरभिधीयते।
तथा ज्ञानाग्निना देही कर्मबन्धात् प्रमुच्यते॥
verses.transliteration:
Yathā hy agniṁ prakāśaṁ ca samibhir abhidhīyate,
tathā jñānāgninā dehī karmabandāt pramucyate.
verses.translation: Just as fire is kindled and its light revealed by fuel, so is the embodied soul liberated from the bondage of karma by the fire of knowledge.

verses.hindi:
अहिंसा सत्यमस्तेयं शौचमिन्द्रियनिग्रहः।
दानं दमो दया शान्तिः सर्वेषां धर्मसाधनम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Ahiṁsā satyam asteyaṁ śaucam indriyannigrahaḥ,
dānaṁ damo dayā śāntiḥ sarveṣāṁ dharmasādhanam.
verses.translation: Non-violence, truthfulness, non-stealing, purity, and control of the senses; charity, self-restraint, compassion, and peace — these are the means of dharma for all beings.

verses.hindi:
कामक्रोधौ तथा लोभं मदमोहौ च मत्सरम्।
त्यक्त्वा सर्वं विकारं हि ज्ञानवान् मुच्यते नरः॥
verses.transliteration:
Kāmakrodhau tathā lobhaṁ madamohau ca matsaram,
tyaktvā sarvaṁ vikāraṁ hi jñānavān mucyate naraḥ.
verses.translation: Having abandoned lust, anger, greed, pride, delusion, and envy — giving up all mental distortions — the wise man is liberated.

verses.hindi:
मम लोकं न पश्यन्ति ये धर्मपरिपालकाः।
ते यान्ति परमं स्थानं यत्र गत्वा न शोचति॥
verses.transliteration:
Mama lokaṁ na paśyanti ye dharmaparipālakāḥ,
te yānti paramaṁ sthānaṁ yatra gatvā na śocati.
verses.translation: Those who uphold dharma never see my realm (the realm of death). They attain the supreme abode, reaching which one grieves no more.

verses.hindi:
न मे प्रियश्चाप्रियो वा कश्चित्
समो हि सर्वेषु च जन्तुषु अहम्।
कर्मानुसारेण फलं ददामि
न मत्कृतं दुःखमिदं हि लोके॥
verses.transliteration:
Na me priyaś cāpriyo vā kaścit
samo hi sarveṣu ca jantuṣu aham,
karmānusāreṇa phalaṁ dadāmi
na matkṛtaṁ duḥkham idaṁ hi loke.
verses.translation: No one is dear or hateful to me; I am equal toward all living beings. I give the fruits according to one's own karma — the suffering in this world is not caused by me.

verses.hindi:
स्वयं कृतं कर्म नरो ह्यनुभवेत्
न कर्मणां लिप्यति कर्ता अन्यः।
तस्मात् सदा कर्म शुभं समाचरेत्
अशुभं कर्म विवर्जयेत्॥
verses.transliteration:
Svayaṁ kṛtaṁ karma naro hy anubhaved
na karmaṇāṁ lipyati kartā anyaḥ,
tasmāt sadā karma śubhaṁ samācaret
aśubhaṁ karma vivarjayed.
verses.translation: A man himself experiences the karma he has created; no other agent is tainted by his actions. Therefore, one should always perform auspicious deeds and shun inauspicious ones.

verses.hindi:
मृत्युना हि सदा लोकः समन्तात् परिवेष्टितः।
जरया व्याधिभिश्चैव दुःखैश्चान्यैः समावृतः॥
verses.transliteration:
Mṛtyunā hi sadā lokaḥ samantāt pariveṣṭitaḥ,
jarayā vyādhibhiś caiva duḥkhaiś cānyaiḥ samāvṛtaḥ.
verses.translation: This world is always encircled by death on all sides, and is surrounded by old age, disease, and manifold sorrows.

verses.hindi:
एतद् विदित्वा मेधावी धर्ममेव समाश्रयेत्।
धर्मो हि एको अनुगच्छति प्राणिनं
सर्वमन्यत् स्वजनबान्धवं च नश्यति॥
verses.transliteration:
Etad viditvā medhāvī dharmam eva samāśrayed,
dharmo hi eko anugacchati prāṇinaṁ
sarvam anyat svajanabāndhavaṁ ca naśyati.
verses.translation: Knowing this, the intelligent person should take refuge in dharma alone. For dharma alone follows a being after death; everything else — family and kinsmen — perishes.

verses.hindi:
न बान्धवाः पुत्रकलत्रमित्राः
न संचितं रत्नधनं च किञ्चित्।
मृतं शरीरं प्रतिगृह्य गच्छन्ति
धर्म एकोऽनुगतः सदैव॥
verses.transliteration:
Na bāndhavāḥ putrakalatramnitrāḥ
na saṁcitaṁ ratnadhanaṁ ca kiñcit,
mṛtaṁ śarīraṁ pratigṛhya gacchanti
dharma eko'nugataḥ sadaiva.
verses.translation: Neither kinsmen, sons, wife, nor friends, nor accumulated jewels and wealth — none accompany the dead body. Only dharma follows one forever.

verses.hindi:
एकः प्रजायते जन्तुरेक एव प्रलीयते।
एकोऽनुभुङ्क्ते सुकृतमेक एव च दुष्कृतम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Ekaḥ prajāyate jantur eka eva pralīyate,
eko'nubhuṅkte sukṛtam eka eva ca duṣkṛtam.
verses.translation: A being is born alone and dies alone. Alone one experiences the fruits of good deeds, and alone one suffers the consequences of evil deeds.

verses.hindi:
अजरं चामरं चैव प्राप्नुवन्ति मनीषिणः।
ये धर्ममनुतिष्ठन्ति सदा शुभपरायणाः॥
verses.transliteration:
Ajaraṁ cāmaraṁ caiva prāpnuvanti manīṣiṇaḥ,
ye dharmam anutiṣṭhanti sadā śubhaparāyaṇāḥ.
verses.translation: The wise who steadfastly follow dharma and are ever devoted to auspicious conduct attain that which is free from old age and death.

verses.hindi:
तस्मात् सर्वप्रयत्नेन धर्ममाचरेत सुव्रताः।
धर्मेण हीनाः पशुभिः समानाः
धर्मेण हीनाः मृतैश्च तुल्याः॥
verses.transliteration:
Tasmāt sarvaprayatnena dharmam ācaret suvratāḥ,
dharmeṇa hīnāḥ paśubhiḥ samānāḥ
dharmeṇa hīnāḥ mṛtaiś ca tulyāḥ.
verses.translation: Therefore, O virtuous ones, practice dharma with all effort. Those devoid of dharma are equal to animals; those without dharma are no different from the dead.

verses.hindi:
सत्यं ब्रूयात् प्रियं ब्रूयात् न ब्रूयात् सत्यमप्रियम्।
एष धर्मः सनातनो यमेन कथितो नृणाम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Satyaṁ brūyāt priyaṁ brūyāt na brūyāt satyam apriyam,
eṣa dharmaḥ sanātano Yamena kathito nṛṇām.
verses.translation: Speak the truth, speak pleasantly, but do not speak truth that is unpleasant (when avoidable). This is the eternal dharma declared by Yama to mankind.

verses.hindi:
परोपकारः पुण्याय पापाय परपीडनम्।
इति यामी वचः श्रेष्ठं मोक्षमार्गप्रदर्शकम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Paropakāraḥ puṇyāya pāpāya parapīḍanam,
iti Yāmī vacaḥ śreṣṭhaṁ mokṣamārgapradarśakam.
verses.translation: Helping others leads to merit, and harming others leads to sin. This is the supreme teaching of Yama, pointing the way to liberation.

verses.hindi:
यमगीतामिमां पुण्यां यः पठेत् श्रद्धयान्वितः।
यमलोकभयं त्यक्त्वा विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥
verses.transliteration:
Yamagītām imāṁ puṇyāṁ yaḥ paṭhet śraddhayānvitaḥ,
Yamaloakbhayaṁ tyaktvā Viṣṇulokaṁ sa gacchati.
verses.translation: He who reads this holy Yama Gita with faith, casting aside the fear of Yama's realm, attains the abode of Vishnu.

verses.hindi:
इति श्रीगरुडपुराणे सारोद्धारे यमगीता
नाम चतुर्दशोऽध्यायः॥
verses.transliteration:
Iti Śrī Garuḍa Purāṇe Sāroddhāre Yamagītā
nāma caturdaśo'dhyāyaḥ.
verses.translation: Thus ends the fourteenth chapter, entitled 'The Yama Gita,' in the Saroddhara of the Shri Garuda Purana.
